Package org.areslib.math

Class EliteShooterMath

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.math.EliteShooterMath
public class EliteShooterMath extends Object
Advanced Shot-On-The-Move mathematical solver ingested from Team 254 (2024).

Calculates exact trajectory kinematics, solving the quadratic equation for time-of-flight while applying gravity and lift compensation. Adapted from FRC to FTC game piece physics.

Students: This is used for aiming mechanisms that need to launch game pieces while the robot is still moving. The math accounts for the robot's velocity so the game piece arrives at the target despite the robot drifting.

    calculateShotOnTheMove(Pose2d robotPose, ChassisSpeeds fieldRelativeSpeeds, double targetXMeters, double targetYMeters, double targetZMeters, double releaseHeightZMeters, double nominalShotSpeedMetersPerSec, double gravityMetersPerSecSq, double liftCoefficient, EliteShooterMath.EliteShooterSetpoint result)
    Mathematically solves the exact shot state needed to hit a 3D target given current robot speeds and constraints.

    • EliteShooterMath

  • Method Details

    • calculateShotOnTheMove

      public static void calculateShotOnTheMove(Pose2d robotPose, ChassisSpeeds fieldRelativeSpeeds, double targetXMeters, double targetYMeters, double targetZMeters, double releaseHeightZMeters, double nominalShotSpeedMetersPerSec, double gravityMetersPerSecSq, double liftCoefficient, EliteShooterMath.EliteShooterSetpoint result)
      Mathematically solves the exact shot state needed to hit a 3D target given current robot speeds and constraints.

      Mathematical Reference: Trajectory compensation solves for t in: ||P_target - (P_robot + V_robot*t)|| = V_shot * t

      Parameters:
      robotPose - Current field-relative robot pose.
      fieldRelativeSpeeds - Current field-relative speeds of the chassis.
      targetXMeters - The X coordinate of the target on the field (meters).
      targetYMeters - The Y coordinate of the target on the field (meters).
      targetZMeters - The Z coordinate (height) of the target on the field (meters).
      releaseHeightZMeters - Height of the robot's shooter mechanism from the floor (meters).
      nominalShotSpeedMetersPerSec - Base shot velocity output limit.
      gravityMetersPerSecSq - Gravity constant (typically 9.81).
      liftCoefficient - Aerodynamic lift coefficient of the game piece (0 for no lift).
      result - Setpoint object to populate (Zero-allocation pattern).
